segments.text: So, this is lecture 1 on December 7th. This is about system of linear equations. So, when
we say system of linear equations, it is basically like one equation or more than one equation.
So, for example, if somebody gives us 3 x is equal to 1 or say x plus 2 y is equal
to 3. These are examples of linear equations, linear because the powers of x and y are no
more than one and we consider this as a system. So, we are looking for common solutions of
these equations. So, there are two ways in which we can think of the system of linear
equation linear equations. One is the geometric way and the other is just one which we are
going to explore more in detail the algebraic way. Both have their advantages as well as
disadvantages. In geometric way, we can apply some geometric intuition to understand
what we mean by the solution of these equations and algebraic way will help us generalize
these things to a higher dimension. So, for example, let us start with a very basic
thing. When somebody says x is equal to 1, what does this represent? Most of them
when we look at this, we will think x is equal to 1 is basically a point. Some
people might say well this is a line, but when we say when what does this represent,
we have to be careful about the ambient space in which we are working. So, the first
question which we should ask is what is the ambient space in which we are considering
this equation. So, for example, if my ambient space is R 1, then x is equal to 1
is simply a point. If my ambient space happens to be R 2, then x is equal to 1
would be aligned. So, the same equation x is equal to 1 represents a totally different
object depending on the ambient space we are in. And when we say R 3, the same
x is equal to 1 will now represent a plane. So, it depends on the context in which
the equation has been written. We will try to answer or we will try to attempt
the solution to that particular equation. So, let us see if we were given these two
equations, since there are two variables we know that we are at least in a two dimensional
world maybe more, but suppose we restrict our attention to R 2 which is the two
dimensional world and then we want to know what is the solution to this system of
equation. So, we want to solve it in a geometric way first. So, 3 x is equal
to 1 in R 2 will represent a line for us something like this x is equal to one third
this is our line and x plus 2 y is equal to 3 is also going to represent a line
for us perhaps something like this. And once we have these two lines we are
looking for a point which is common to both of them and this point will be called
a solution for us. Now, this is a geometric picture what if we really want to know
what exactly is the solution that means, what we want to know is what are the values
of x and y that can help that lie on both these lines. So, we need some
algebraic way to find our solutions. In this particular case the answer is
fairly easy because we already have x as one third and when we plug in for y
we will be able to find what is the value of y. So, for x is equal to one third y
would be 3 minus x over 2 and this will give us 8 over 6 which is four thirds.
So, y would be four third in which case we can claim that we have found our point
we have found our solution which is namely one third and four thirds. So, this is
what we would mean by a solution of this system of equation that this system
was considered in R 2. Now, the question is this is the only solution.
So, we may ask ourselves given any system of equations is it always the case that
we will have only one solution, but it possibly have more than one solution,
is it possible that it has no solution and what turns out to be that looking
through geometry it helps us understand many situations fairly easily and that
will lay the groundwork for how things are going to be later on.
So, for example, if we are in R 2 we understand that linear equations are
going to represent a line. So, we may have a situation like this where we
have been given equations in present two lines and they turn out to have
an unique solution or we may have a situation where we are given two lines
which turn out to be parallel to each other and they have no solution or we
may have a situation where we have two lines rather I should say we have
two equations which represent the same straight line in which case we have
infinitely many solutions.
So, question is can there be a situation which is different from these three
that means can we have a situation where we have more than one solution,
but finitely many solutions in the sense can it happen that I have two
straight lines which can meet at more than one point, but not all of them
and a simple thought at least geometrically one can intuitively say that
if a straight line passes through two points then it is a unique straight line.
So, if I have another straight line through the same two points then it must
be the straight line I started with. So, the situation here is that given a
system of equations I will either have only one solution, no solution or
infinitely many solutions. So, question is can this be generalized to higher
dimensions for example if we were in R 3 what would happen to the number
of solutions. So, let us try our luck in R 3. So, in R 3 or in third
dimension suppose I give suppose we have bunch of equations say x plus x
minus y plus z is 1 2 x minus 3 y plus 7 z is 2 suppose I have two
equations in R 3 you can see that each of these equations will
represent a linear plane. So, these are basically two planes in R 3.
Now, the question is if I am given two planes in R 3. So, imagine this is
one plane. So, imagine this is one plane let us talk about the
floor or the table it is one plane and if we have another plane imagine
this is one plane and here is another plane. If we are given two
planes then they might intersect and once they intersect they are going to
intersect along a line. So, think of two pages in a book they meet at the
binding or the spine of the book. So, a situation like this may happen
and you have a plane and you have another plane and both of them are
meeting in a line. So, they both meet in a line and that means we are
in the situation of infinitely many solutions. What if we are given two
planes which do not meet something like this parallel planes just think of
the roof and the floor they do not meet. So, this corresponds to the situation
when we have no solution and of course, two planes can be on top of
each other representing the same plane like this in which case we have
infinitely many solutions. Now, the question is is it possible that planes
can have a unique solution. If you think two planes wherever they intersect
they will intersect at more than one point just think of like cutting the
butter with a knife. But if I give you a third plane to work with let us say
another plane I will just make up some numbers hopefully they will work.
But if I give you three planes then is it possible for us to see if they all
meet at a point. Now, imagine the corner of a room you have the wall
that front the opposite wall and the roof they all are meeting in one
point. So, it is possible that when we are given three planes if they all
intersect it is possible that we may end up having a solution with a
solution which is unique. So, this tells us that whether we go from R2
to R3 or R3 to R4 the number of solutions seem to be either one
infinitely many or zero. Will this situation carry on later on for
higher dimensions also like R4, R5 and we shall explore that but if I
may jump the gun a bit. In fact, we will see that we can prove that
even in R3, R4, R5 any Rn the number of solutions the number of
solutions of linear system of linear equations 1, 0 infinitely many.
Now, this is something which we are guessing right now but later on we
shall prove that this would be the case and the reasoning comes from
basically understanding these things in a more algebraic manner.
Now, our aim is to try to solve the system of linear equations. So, we
will try to find a way of solving these things in a purely algebraic
manner and then we will try to understand how to write the solutions
to these things. First of all let us get one thing cleared up right
away. Suppose we are given this equation or system of equation I
should say something like this. Now, x and y are variables out here.
So, whether I say x minus 2 y equal to 3 or suppose I write a
minus 2 v is equal to 3 and 2 a plus 3 v is equal to 7. If you
look at these two systems of equation they are identical except
for the fact that in one case I have used x and y as variables in
another case a and b are my variables but nonetheless whatever the
solution out here whatever we call x equal to same we will hold
here for a is equal to. So, part of the matter is it does not matter
what variables are used the things which are really playing the
role are these numbers 2, 3, 7 these coefficients. So, what we
do is instead of worrying about this we will put this information
in a matrix. So, we will say something like 1 minus 2 2 3. So,
I have isolated the coefficients of all the variables and then I
also incorporate the right hand side 3, 7 and then we just put
a vertical bar here. So, that it represents that the column of
the right hand side represents the right the right hand side of
the equation. This is what we call as an augmented matrix.
So, we can think of augmented matrix consisting of two things.
This is what the coefficients are and this is what we call as the
right hand side vector. So, once we have this let us give these guys
some names. So, anytime I see augmented matrix the left part of
it is a matrix a which I will call the coefficient matrix and
the right hand side is the vector b which we will call as our
right hand side vector or sometimes we call it as a constant
vector. So, any augmented any system of equation can be written
in terms of its augmented matrix and given any augmented
matrix we can convert it back to a system of equations. So,
this is just a notation we are going to use for some time
and then we will see how things work out for us.
Still the problem is we have not seen a way to solve the
equation, but before we venture into solving the equation let us
explore something slightly different which we will eventually
tie up with this idea. So, let me call something about a row
picture and a column picture. These things are we do not have
to be important later on in the course may be not right
now, but at least we should start thinking in these terms
and it will be beneficial for us. So, for example, given
the system of equation x minus 2 y equal to 3 and 2 x plus 3 y
is equal to 7. If I look it from the perspective of rows
this is one row this is another row. If I look it from this
perspective solving the system of equation means I am trying
to find the intersection of two lines this is what would be
the row picture for this system of equation. One may say
well what is new in that suddenly there is nothing new in
this it is just the way of looking at thing, but we can
also think of this in a different way. For example, if
we concentrate on this system of equation and think in
terms of vectors I can write this system as x times 1 2
and look at this column representing y, y times minus 2 3
and on the right hand side we have 3 7. Now, can we say
that this is the same system of equations, but we are now
viewing it in a slightly different manner which I like to
call as a column picture. What this is asking us is it
possible for me to represent this vector this column
vector as a linear combination of these two vectors.
So, the column picture for this is can the vector 3 7 be
written as a linear combination 1 2 and minus 2 3.
Now, one may wonder what is the use of looking in terms
of a column picture and as I mentioned earlier right now
we may not see a big use, but we are just trying to
get used to a language of linear combination which we
manage to us. One thing I should point out here is this
picture was in R 2 and here also we are dealing in R 2
because we have two dimensional vectors and they are asking
can this two dimensional vector be written as a combination
of two other vectors in two dimensions. However, the
row picture and column picture may not be from the
same dimension. For example, it may happen and suppose
we are given these equations say x minus y plus z is
equal to 21 3 x plus y minus 3 z is 0 suppose I have been
given this system of equations from the if I want the
row picture of it row picture is basically looking at
the system as rows. So, we are talking about
intersection of two planes these two planes live inside
R 3 whereas, if I want the column picture of this
something different happens. The column picture of this
would be x times the vector 1 3 y times the vector
minus 1 1 and z times the vector 1 minus 3 is equal to
21 0. If you look at the column picture it is asking
can this vector can this right hand side vector be
written as a linear combination of these three
vectors, but all these vectors are in R 2. So, as you can
see the row picture and the column picture are from
two different worlds even though they are referring to
the same system of equations. Will this be advantageous
for us at times it will be advantageous for us to
change our perspective and we might be able to answer
some things in here and that will shed light on how
the answer will look like in the case of the row picture.
So, we will keep these ideas in mind we will keep
this particular phrase in mind linear combination as
it will keep on popping up quite a few times in this
course and we will try to see if this can help us
understand solving system of equations in a nice manner.
So, we have learnt bunch of things we know that
even a system of equation I can view it as a row
picture I can view as in terms of columns and
algebraically I can represent it as represented as
augmented matrix.
So, now let us try to see if we can solve a system
of equation. So, let us try our luck with something
very very simple let me change it let me just put it
like this let me call it x equal to 1 y equal to
3. Now, I have deliberately written things in a weird
manner so that we can understand this is like 0
times y and this is 0 times x. If you look at this
system of equation again let us assume we are
working in R 2 can we say that this system has a
solution x is equal to 1 y is equal to 3. So, in
some sense we have nothing to do we do not really
need to do any thing and we can still get the
answer. So, this would be in some sense our ideal
situation when we would when we can have a matrix
when we can have an augmented matrix which looks
like this. So, if our augmented matrix looks like
this we can easily read out the solution because
I can convert it back to this and read out the
solution. So, in some sense this is what we call
as our dream augmented matrix when this is not
a technical term something which I have just made
In fact, there is a name for it the name is
basically we will call this kind of a matrix as
row reduced echelon form. So, in some sense we
have want to target row reduced echelon form
but the question is given a general matrix how
can I achieve this and in order to do that
we will learn something called row operations.
